      "text": "Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document.\n\n## Summary: Analysis of Indicator Support in Pandas Merge Functions\n\nBased on my exploration of the pandas `merge.py` file, here is a detailed analysis of what needs to be modified to add indicator support to `merge_asof`:\n\n---\n\n### 1. **merge_asof Function Signature (Line 659-674)**\n\n```python\ndef merge_asof(\n    left: DataFrame | Series,\n    right: DataFrame | Series,\n    on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    left_index: bool = False,\n    right_index: bool = False,\n    by=None,\n    left_by=None,\n    right_by=None,\n    suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n    tolerance: int | datetime.timedelta | None = None,\n    allow_exact_matches: bool = True,\n    direction: str = \"backward\",\n) -> DataFrame:\n```\n\n**Key observation**: The `indicator` parameter is **completely missing** from the function signature. This is the fundamental change needed.\n\n---\n\n### 2. **_AsOfMerge Class __init__ Signature (Line 2318-2335)**\n\n```python\ndef __init__(\n    self,\n    left: DataFrame | Series,\n    right: DataFrame | Series,\n    on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    left_index: bool = False,\n    right_index: bool = False,\n    by=None,\n    left_by=None,\n    right_by=None,\n    suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"__y\"),\n    how: Literal[\"asof\"] = \"asof\",\n    tolerance=None,\n    allow_exact_matches: bool = True,\n    direction: str = \"backward\",\n) -> None:\n```\n\n**Key observation**: The `indicator` parameter is **missing** from `_AsOfMerge.__init__()`. This class needs to accept and pass the indicator parameter to its parent class.\n\n---\n\n### 3. **_OrderedMerge Class __init__ Signature (Line 2253-2265)**\n\n```python\ndef __init__(\n    self,\n    left: DataFrame | Series,\n    right: DataFrame | Series,\n    on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n    left_index: bool = False,\n    right_index: bool = False,\n    suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n    fill_method: str | None = None,\n    how: JoinHow | Literal[\"asof\"] = \"outer\",\n) -> None:\n```\n\n**Key observation**: `_OrderedMerge` also **lacks the `indicator` parameter**. It needs to pass it through to `_MergeOperation.__init__()`.\n\n---\n\n### 4. **_OrderedMerge.get_result() Method (Line 2281-2307)**\n\n```python\ndef get_result(self) -> DataFrame:\n    join_index, left_indexer, right_indexer = self._get_join_info()\n\n    left_join_indexer: npt.NDArray[np.intp] | None\n    right_join_indexer: npt.NDArray[np.intp] | None\n\n    if self.fill_method == \"ffill\":\n        if left_indexer is None:\n            left_join_indexer = None\n        else:\n            left_join_indexer = libjoin.ffill_indexer(left_indexer)\n        if right_indexer is None:\n            right_join_indexer = None\n        else:\n            right_join_indexer = libjoin.ffill_indexer(right_indexer)\n    elif self.fill_method is None:\n        left_join_indexer = left_indexer\n        right_join_indexer = right_indexer\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"fill_method must be 'ffill' or None\")\n\n    result = self._reindex_and_concat(\n        join_index, left_join_indexer, right_join_indexer\n    )\n    self._maybe_add_join_keys(result, left_indexer, right_indexer)\n\n    return result\n```\n\n**Key observation**: `_OrderedMerge.get_result()` **does NOT handle indicator**. It doesn't call `_indicator_pre_merge()` or `_indicator_post_merge()`. Since `_AsOfMerge` inherits from `_OrderedMerge`, it would need to override `get_result()` to add indicator support.\n\n---\n\n### 5. **How Indicator is Handled in _MergeOperation.get_result() (Line 1136-1159)**\n\n```python\ndef get_result(self) -> DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"\n    Execute the merge.\n    \"\"\"\n    if self.indicator:\n        self.left, self.right = self._indicator_pre_merge(self.left, self.right)\n\n    join_index, left_indexer, right_indexer = self._get_join_info()\n\n    result = self._reindex_and_concat(join_index, left_indexer, right_indexer)\n\n    if self.indicator:\n        result = self._indicator_post_merge(result)\n\n    self._maybe_add_join_keys(result, left_indexer, right_indexer)\n\n    self._maybe_restore_index_levels(result)\n\n    return result.__finalize__(\n        types.SimpleNamespace(\n            input_objs=[self.left, self.right], left=self.left, right=self.right\n        ),\n        method=\"merge\",\n    )\n```\n\n**Key pattern**: The base `_MergeOperation` class follows a clear pattern:\n1. **Before merge**: Call `_indicator_pre_merge()` to add temporary indicator columns\n2. **Perform merge**: Execute the join logic\n3. **After merge**: Call `_indicator_post_merge()` to create the final indicator column\n\n---\n\n### 6. **_indicator_pre_merge() Method (Line 1174-1206)**\n\n```python\n@final\ndef _indicator_pre_merge(\n    self, left: DataFrame, right: DataFrame\n) -> tuple[DataFrame, DataFrame]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Add one indicator column to each of the left and right inputs.\n\n    These columns are used to produce another column in the output of the\n    merge, indicating for each row of the output whether it was produced\n    using the left, right or both inputs.\n    \"\"\"\n    columns = left.columns.union(right.columns)\n\n    for i in [\"_left_indicator\", \"_right_indicator\"]:\n        if i in columns:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"Cannot use `indicator=True` option when \"\n                f\"data contains a column named {i}\"\n            )\n    if self._indicator_name in columns:\n        raise ValueError(\n            \"Cannot use name of an existing column for indicator column\"\n        )\n\n    left = left.copy(deep=False)\n    right = right.copy(deep=False)\n\n    left[\"_left_indicator\"] = 1\n    left[\"_left_indicator\"] = left[\"_left_indicator\"].astype(\"int8\")\n\n    right[\"_right_indicator\"] = 2\n    right[\"_right_indicator\"] = right[\"_right_indicator\"].astype(\"int8\")\n\n    return left, right\n```\n\n**What it does**: \n- Adds temporary `_left_indicator` column to left (value: 1)\n- Adds temporary `_right_indicator` column to right (value: 2)\n- These are shallow copies to avoid modifying originals\n- Validates that the columns don't already exist\n\n---\n\n### 7. **_indicator_post_merge() Method (Line 1209-1228)**\n\n```python\n@final\ndef _indicator_post_merge(self, result: DataFrame) -> DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"\n    Add an indicator column to the merge result.\n\n    This column indicates for each row of the output whether it was produced using\n    the left, right or both inputs.\n    \"\"\"\n    result[\"_left_indicator\"] = result[\"_left_indicator\"].fillna(0)\n    result[\"_right_indicator\"] = result[\"_right_indicator\"].fillna(0)\n\n    result[self._indicator_name] = Categorical(\n        (result[\"_left_indicator\"] + result[\"_right_indicator\"]),\n        categories=[1, 2, 3],\n    )\n    result[self._indicator_name] = result[\n        self._indicator_name\n    ].cat.rename_categories([\"left_only\", \"right_only\", \"both\"])\n\n    result = result.drop(labels=[\"_left_indicator\", \"_right_indicator\"], axis=1)\n    return result\n```\n\n**What it does**:\n- Fills NaN values in indicator columns with 0\n- Combines the two indicator columns (1 + 2 = 3 for \"both\", 1 alone for \"left_only\", 2 alone for \"right_only\")\n- Creates a categorical column with proper labels\n- Drops the temporary indicator columns\n\n**_indicator_name Property (Line 1162-1171)**:\n```python\n@cache_readonly\ndef _indicator_name(self) -> str | None:\n    if isinstance(self.indicator, str):\n        return self.indicator\n    elif isinstance(self.indicator, bool):\n        return \"_merge\" if self.indicator else None\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\n            \"indicator option can only accept boolean or string arguments\"\n        )\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary of Required Modifications\n\nTo add indicator support to `merge_asof`, the following changes are needed:\n\n| Component | Current State | Required Changes |\n|-----------|---------------|------------------|\n| **merge_asof() signature** | Missing `indicator` parameter | Add `indicator: str \\| bool = False` parameter |\n| **merge_asof() body** | Doesn't pass indicator to _AsOfMerge | Pass `indicator=indicator` to _AsOfMerge constructor |\n| **_AsOfMerge.__init__()** | Missing `indicator` parameter | Add `indicator: str \\| bool = False` parameter; pass to parent |\n| **_OrderedMerge.__init__()** | Missing `indicator` parameter | Add `indicator: str \\| bool = False` parameter; pass to _MergeOperation |\n| **_AsOfMerge inheritance** | Inherits from _OrderedMerge | May need to override `get_result()` if _OrderedMerge doesn't handle indicator |\n| **_OrderedMerge.get_result()** | No indicator handling | Add indicator pre/post merge calls (or pass to parent) |\n\nThe key insight is that `_MergeOperation` already has the infrastructure for indicator handling (`_indicator_pre_merge()` and `_indicator_post_merge()`), but `_OrderedMerge` (and thus `_AsOfMerge`) don't use them. The solution is either:\n\n1. **Option A**: Override `get_result()` in `_AsOfMerge` to call the indicator methods (similar to `_MergeOperation`)\n2. **Option B**: Modify `_OrderedMerge.get_result()` to call the indicator methods, making it available to both `_OrderedMerge` and `_AsOfMerge`\n\nOption B is cleaner as it would automatically support indicator in both `merge_ordered()` and `merge_asof()`.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the pandas merge.py file to understand:\n1. The complete signature of the merge_asof function (around line 440)\n2. The complete __init__ signature of _AsOfMerge class (around line 1962)\n3. The complete __init__ signature of _OrderedMerge class (around line 1897)\n4. The get_result() method of _OrderedMerge (around line 1925)\n5. How indicator is handled in _MergeOperation's get_result() (around line 882)\n6. How _indicator_pre_merge and _indicator_post_merge work in _MergeOperation\n\nUse sg_read_file to read these specific sections and provide a summary of what needs to be modified to add indicator support to merge_asof.",
  "status": "completed",

if we can groupby the rhs\\n478:     # then we can get vastly better perf\\n479:     if all(item in right.columns for item in by):\\n480:         rby = right.groupby(by, sort=False)\\n481: \\n482:     for key, lhs in lby._grouper.get_iterator(lby._selected_obj):\\n483:         if rby is None:\\n484:             rhs = right\\n485:         else:\\n486:             try:\\n487:                 rhs = right.take(rby.indices[key])\\n488:             except KeyError:\\n489:                 # key doesn't exist in left\\n490:                 lcols = lhs.columns.tolist()\\n491:                 cols = lcols + [r for r in right.columns if r not in set(lcols)]\\n492:                 merged = lhs.reindex(columns=cols)\\n493:                 merged.index = range(len(merged))\\n494:                 pieces.append(merged)\\n495:                 continue\\n496: \\n497:         merged = merge_pieces(lhs, rhs)\\n498: \\n499:         # make sure join keys are in the merged\\n500:         # TODO, should merge_pieces do this?\\n501:         merged[by] = key\\n502: \\n503:         pieces.append(merged)\\n504: \\n505:     # preserve the original order\\n506:     # if we have a missing piece this can be reset\\n507:     from pandas.core.reshape.concat import concat\\n508: \\n509:     result = concat(pieces, ignore_index=True)\\n510:     result = result.reindex(columns=pieces[0].columns)\\n511:     return result, lby\\n512: \\n513: \\n514: @set_module(\\\"pandas\\\")\\n515: def merge_ordered(\\n516:     left: DataFrame | Series,\\n517:     right: DataFrame | Series,\\n518:     on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n519:     left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n520:     right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n521: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pandas-dev/pandas@5b19efbb472e64f9fa5a66b5779fcbe0bad3670a/-/blob/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py%3FL440-520\"}"

TODO: transformations??\\n940: class _MergeOperation:\\n941:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n942:     Perform a database (SQL) merge operation between two DataFrame or Series\\n943:     objects using either columns as keys or their row indexes\\n944:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n945: \\n946:     _merge_type = \\\"merge\\\"\\n947:     how: JoinHow | Literal[\\\"asof\\\"]\\n948:     on: IndexLabel | None\\n949:     # left_on/right_on may be None when passed, but in validate_specification\\n950:     #  get replaced with non-None.\\n951: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pandas-dev/pandas@5b19efbb472e64f9fa5a66b5779fcbe0bad3670a/-/blob/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py%3FL880-950\"}"

  a          1\\n790:     1   5        b          6\\n791:     2  10        c          7\\n792: \\n793:     We can use indexed DataFrames as well.\\n794: \\n795:     >>> left = pd.DataFrame({\\\"left_val\\\": [\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"]}, index=[1, 5, 10])\\n796:     >>> left\\n797:        left_val\\n798:     1         a\\n799:     5         b\\n800:     10        c\\n801: \\n802:     >>> right = pd.DataFrame({\\\"right_val\\\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]}, index=[1, 2, 3, 6, 7])\\n803:     >>> right\\n804:        right_val\\n805:     1          1\\n806:     2          2\\n807:     3          3\\n808:     6          6\\n809:     7          7\\n810: \\n811:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True)\\n812:        left_val  right_val\\n813:     1         a          1\\n814:     5         b          3\\n815:     10        c          7\\n816: \\n817:     Here is a real-world times-series example\\n818: \\n819:     >>> quotes = pd.DataFrame(\\n820:     ...     {\\n821:     ...         \\\"time\\\": [\\n822:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\\\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.023\\\"),\\n823:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\\\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.023\\\"),\\n824:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\\\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.030\\\"),\\n825:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\\\"2016-05-25     ...             \\\"AAPL\\\",\\n838:     ...             \\\"GOOG\\\",\\n839:     ...             \\\"MSFT\\\",\\n840:     ...         ],\\n841:     ...         \\\"bid\\\": [720.50, 51.95, 51.97, 51.99, 720.50, 97.99, 720.50, 52.01],\\n842:     ...         \\\"ask\\\": [720.93, 51.96, 51.98, 52.00, 720.93, 98.01, 720.88, 52.03],\\n843:     ...     }\\n844:     ... )\\n845:     >>> quotes\\n846:                          time ticker     bid     ask\\n847:     0 2016-05-25 13:30:00.023   GOOG  720.50  720.93\\n848:     1 2016-05-25 13:30:00.023   MSFT   51.95   51.96\\n849:     2 "{\"content\":\"2180:     left : MultiIndex\\n2181:         left index\\n2182:     right : MultiIndex\\n2183:         right index\\n2184:     dropped_level_names : str array\\n2185:         list of non-common level names\\n2186:     join_index : Index\\n2187:         the index of the join between the\\n2188:         common levels of left and right\\n2189:     lindexer : np.ndarray[np.intp]\\n2190:         left indexer\\n2191:     rindexer : np.ndarray[np.intp]\\n2192:         right indexer\\n2193: \\n2194:     Returns\\n2195:     -------\\n2196:     levels : list of Index\\n2197:         levels of combined multiindexes\\n2198:     labels : np.ndarray[np.intp]\\n2199:         labels of combined multiindexes\\n2200:     names : List[Hashable]\\n2201:         names of combined multiindex levels\\n2202: \\n2203:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2204: \\n2205:     def _convert_to_multiindex(index: Index) -> MultiIndex:\\n2206:         if isinstance(index, MultiIndex):\\n2207:             return index\\n2208:         else:\\n2209:             return MultiIndex.from_arrays([index._values], names=[index.name])\\n2210: \\n2211:     # For multi-multi joins with one overlapping level,\\n2212:     # the returned index if of type Index\\n2213:     # Assure that join_index is of type MultiIndex\\n2214:     # so that dropped levels can be appended\\n2215:     join_index = _convert_to_multiindex(join_index)\\n2216: \\n2217:     join_levels = join_index.levels\\n2218:     join_codes = join_index.codes\\n2219:     join_names = join_index.names\\n2220: \\n2221:     # Iterate through the levels that must be restored\\n2222:     for dropped_level_name in dropped_level_names:\\n2223:         if dropped_level_name in left.names:\\n2224:             idx = left\\n2225:             indexer = lindexer\\n2226:         else:\\n2227:             idx = right\\n2228:             indexer = rindexer\\n2229: \\n2230:         # The index of the level name to be restored\\n2231:         name_idx = idx.names.index(dropped_level_name)\\n2232: \\n2233:         restore_levels = idx.levels[name_idx]\\n2234:         # Inject -1 in the codes list where a join was not possible\\n2235:         # IOW indexer[i]=-1\\n2236:         codes = idx.codes[name_idx]\\n2237:         if indexer is None:\\n2238:             restore_codes = codes\\n2239:         else:\\n2240:             restore_codes = algos.take_nd(codes, indexer, fill_value=-1)\\n2241: \\n2242:         # Use + operator: FrozenList.__add__ returns FrozenList, unpacking returns list\\n2243:         join_levels = join_levels + [restore_levels]  # noqa: RUF005\\n2244:         join_codes = join_codes + [restore_codes]  # noqa: RUF005\\n2245:         join_names = join_names + [dropped_level_name]  # noqa: RUF005\\n2246: \\n2247:     return join_levels, join_codes, join_names\\n2248: \\n2249: \\n2250: class _OrderedMerge(_MergeOperation):\\n2251:     _merge_type = \\\"ordered_merge\\\"\\n2252: \\n2253:     def __init__(\\n2254:         self,\\n2255:         left: DataFrame | Series,\\n2256:         right: DataFrame | Series,\\n2257:         on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n2258:         left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n2259:         right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n2260:         left_index: bool = False,\\n2261:         right_index: bool = False,\\n2262:         suffixes: Suffixes = (\\\"_x\\\", \\\"_y\\\"),\\n2263:         fill_method: str | None = None,\\n2264:         how: JoinHow | Literal[\\\"asof\\\"] = \\\"outer\\\",\\n2265:     ) -> None:\\n2266:         self.fill_method = fill_method\\n2267:         _MergeOperation.__init__(\\n2268:             self,\\n2269:             left,\\n2270:             right,\\n2271:             on=on,\\n2272:             left_on=left_on,\\n2273:             left_index=left_index,\\n2274:             right_index=right_index,\\n2275:             right_on=right_on,\\n2276:             how=how,\\n2277:             suffixes=suffixes,\\n2278:             sort=True,  # factorize sorts\\n2279:         )\\n2280: \\n2281: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pandas-dev/pandas@5b19efbb472e64f9fa5a66b5779fcbe0bad3670a/-/blob/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py%3FL2180-2280\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"695: \\n696:     Parameters\\n697:     ----------\\n698:     left : DataFrame or named Series\\n699:         First pandas object to merge.\\n700:     right : DataFrame or named Series\\n701:         Second pandas object to merge.\\n702:     on : label\\n703:         Field name to join on. Must be found in both DataFrames.\\n704:         The data MUST be in ascending order. Furthermore this must be\\n705:         a numeric column, such as datetimelike, integer, or float. ``on``\\n706:         or ``left_on`` / ``right_on`` must be given.\\n707:     left_on : label\\n708:         Field name to join on in left DataFrame. If specified, sort the left\\n709:         DataFrame by this column in ascending order before merging.\\n710:     right_on : label\\n711:         Field name to join on in right DataFrame. If specified, sort the right\\n712:         DataFrame by this column in ascending order before merging.\\n713:     left_index : bool\\n714:         Use the index of the left DataFrame as the join key.\\n715:     right_index : bool\\n716:         Use the index of the right DataFrame as the join key.\\n717:     by : column name or list of column names\\n718:         Match on these columns before performing merge operation. It is not required\\n719:         to sort by these columns.\\n720:     left_by : column name\\n721:         Field names to match on in the left DataFrame.\\n722:     right_by : column name\\n723:         Field names to match on in the right DataFrame.\\n724:     suffixes : 2-length sequence (tuple, list, ...)\\n725:         Suffix to apply to overlapping column names in the left and right\\n726:         side, respectively.\\n727:     tolerance : int or timedelta, optional, default None\\n728:         Select asof tolerance within this range; must be compatible\\n729:         with the merge index.\\n730:     allow_exact_matches : bool, default True\\n731: \\n732:         - If True, allow matching with the same 'on' value\\n733:           (i.e. less-than-or-equal-to / greater-than-or-equal-to)\\n734:         - If False, don't match the same 'on' value\\n735:           (i.e., strictly less-than / strictly greater-than).\\n736: \\n737:     direction : 'backward' (default), 'forward', or 'nearest'\\n738:         Whether to search for prior, subsequent, or closest matches.\\n739: \\n740:     Returns\\n741:     -------\\n742:     DataFrame\\n743:         A DataFrame of the two merged objects, containing all rows from the\\n744:         left DataFrame and the nearest matches from the right DataFrame.\\n745: \\n746:     See Also\\n747:     --------\\n748:     merge : Merge with a database-style join.\\n749:     merge_ordered : Merge with optional filling/interpolation.\\n750: \\n751:     Examples\\n752:     --------\\n753:     >>> left = pd.DataFrame({\\\"a\\\": [1, 5, 10], \\\"left_val\\\": [\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"]})\\n754:     >>> left\\n755:         a left_val\\n756:     0   1        a\\n757:     1   5        b\\n758:     2  10        c\\n759: \\n760:     >>> right = pd.DataFrame({\\\"a\\\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \\\"right_val\\\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\\n761:     >>> right\\n762:        a  right_val\\n763:     0  1          1\\n764:     1  2          2\\n765:     2  3          3\\n766:     3  6          6\\n767:     4  7          7\\n768: \\n769:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\\\"a\\\")\\n770:         a left_val  right_val\\n771:     0   1        a          1\\n772:     1   5        b          3\\n773:     2  10        c          7\\n774: \\n775:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\\\"a\\\", allow_exact_matches=False)\\n776:         a left_val  right_val\\n777:     0   1        a        NaN\\n778:     1   5        b        3.0\\n779:     2  10        c        7.0\\n780: \\n781:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\\\"a\\\", direction=\\\"forward\\\")\\n782:         a left_val  right_val\\n783:     0   1        a        1.0\\n784:     1   5        b        6.0\\n785:     2  10        c        NaN\\n786: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pandas-dev/pandas@5b19efbb472e64f9fa5a66b5779fcbe0bad3670a/-/blob/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py%3FL695-785\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1000:                 f\\\"right_index parameter must be of type bool, not {type(right_index)}\\\"\\n1001:             )\\n1002: \\n1003:         # GH 40993: raise when merging between different levels; enforced in 2.0\\n1004:         if _left.columns.nlevels != _right.columns.nlevels:\\n1005:             msg = (\\n1006:                 \\\"Not allowed to merge between different levels. \\\"\\n1007:                 f\\\"({_left.columns.nlevels} levels on the left, \\\"\\n1008:                 f\\\"{_right.columns.nlevels} on the right)\\\"\\n1009:             )\\n1010:             raise MergeError(msg)\\n1011: \\n1012:         self.left_on, self.right_on = self._validate_left_right_on(left_on, right_on)\\n1013: \\n1014:         (\\n1015:             self.left_join_keys,\\n1016:             self.right_join_keys,\\n1017:             self.join_names,\\n1018:             left_drop,\\n1019:             right_drop,\\n1020:         ) = self._get_merge_keys()\\n1021: \\n1022:         if left_drop:\\n1023:             self.left = self.left._drop_labels_or_levels(left_drop)\\n1024: \\n1025:         if right_drop:\\n1026:             self.right = self.right._drop_labels_or_levels(right_drop)\\n1027: \\n1028:         self._maybe_require_matching_dtypes(self.left_join_keys, self.right_join_keys)\\n1029:         self._validate_tolerance(self.left_join_keys)\\n1030: \\n1031:         # validate the merge keys dtypes. We may need to coerce\\n1032:         # to avoid incompatible dtypes\\n1033:         self._maybe_coerce_merge_keys()\\n1034: \\n1035:         # If argument passed to validate,\\n1036:         # check if columns specified as unique\\n1037:         # are in fact unique.\\n1038:         if validate is not None:\\n1039:             self._validate_validate_kwd(validate)\\n1040: \\n1041:     @final\\n1042:     def _validate_how(\\n1043:         self, how: JoinHow | Literal[\\\"left_anti\\\", \\\"right_anti\\\", \\\"asof\\\"]\\n1044:     ) -> tuple[JoinHow | Literal[\\\"asof\\\"], bool]:\\n1045:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1046:         Validate the 'how' parameter and return the actual join type and whether\\n1047:         this is an anti join.\\n1048:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1049:         # GH 59435: raise when \\\"how\\\" is not a valid Merge type\\n1050:         merge_type = {\\n1051:             \\\"left\\\",\\n1052:             \\\"right\\\",\\n1053:             \\\"inner\\\",\\n1054:             \\\"outer\\\",\\n1055:             \\\"left_anti\\\",\\n1056:             \\\"right_anti\\\",\\n1057:             \\\"cross\\\",\\n1058:             \\\"asof\\\",\\n1059:         }\\n1060:         if how not in merge_type:\\n1061:             raise ValueError(\\n1062:                 f\\\"'{how}' is not a valid Merge type: \\\"\\n1063:                 f\\\"left, right, inner, outer, left_anti, right_anti, cross, asof\\\"\\n1064:             )\\n1065:         anti_join = False\\n1066:         if how in {\\\"left_anti\\\", \\\"right_anti\\\"}:\\n1067:             how = how.split(\\\"_\\\")[0]  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1068:             anti_join = True\\n1069:         how = cast(\\\"JoinHow | Literal['asof']\\\", how)\\n1070:         return how, anti_join\\n1071: \\n1072:     def _maybe_require_matching_dtypes(\\n1073:         self, left_join_keys: list[ArrayLike], right_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]\\n1074:     ) -> None:\\n1075:         # Overridden by AsOfMerge\\n1076:         pass\\n1077: \\n1078:     def _validate_tolerance(self, left_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]) -> None:\\n1079:         # Overridden by AsOfMerge\\n1080:         pass\\n1081: \\n1082:     @final\\n1083:     def _reindex_and_concat(\\n1084:         self,\\n1085:         join_index: Index,\\n1086:         left_indexer: npt.NDArray[np.intp] | None,\\n1087:         right_indexer: npt.NDArray[np.intp] | None,\\n1088:     ) -> DataFrame:\\n1089:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1090:         reindex along index and concat along columns.\\n1091:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1092:         # Take views so we do not alter the originals\\n1093:         left = self.left[:]\\n1094:         right = self.right[:]\\n1095: \\n1096:         llabels, rlabels = _items_overlap_with_suffix(\\n1097:             self.left._info_axis, self.right._info_axis, self.suffixes\\n1098:         )\\n1099: \\n1100:         if left_indexer is not None and not is_range_indexer(left_indexer, len(left)):\\n1101:             # Pinning the index here (and in the right code just below) is not\\n1102:             #  necessary, but makes the `.take` more performant if we have e.g.\\n1103:             #  a MultiIndex for left.index.\\n1104:             lmgr = left._mgr.reindex_indexer(\\n1105:                 join_index,\\n1106:                 left_indexer,\\n1107:                 axis=1,\\n1108:                 only_slice=True,\\n1109:                 allow_dups=True,\\n1110:                 use_na_proxy=True,\\n1111:             )\\n1112:             left = left._constructor_from_mgr(lmgr, axes=lmgr.axes)\\n1113:         left.index = join_index\\n1114: \\n1115:         if right_indexer is not None and not is_range_indexer(\\n1116:             right_indexer, len(right)\\n1117:         ):\\n1118:             rmgr = right._mgr.reindex_indexer(\\n1119:                 join_index,\\n1120:                 right_indexer,\\n1121:                 axis=1,\\n1122:                 only_slice=True,\\n1123:                 allow_dups=True,\\n1124:                 use_na_proxy=True,\\n1125:             )\\n1126:             right = right._constructor_from_mgr(rmgr, axes=rmgr.axes)\\n1127:         right.index = join_index\\n1128: \\n1129:         from pandas import concat\\n1130: \\n1131:         left.columns = llabels\\n1132:         right.columns = rlabels\\n1133:         result = concat([left, right], axis=1)\\n1134:         return result\\n1135: \\n1136:     def get_result(self) -> DataFrame:\\n1137:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1138:         Execute the merge.\\n1139:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1140:         if self.indicator:\\n1141:             self.left, self.right = self._indicator_pre_merge(self.left, self.right)\\n1142: \\n1143:         join_index, left_indexer, right_indexer = self._get_join_info()\\n1144: \\n1145:         result = self._reindex_and_concat(join_index, left_indexer, right_indexer)\\n1146: \\n1147:         if self.indicator:\\n1148:             result = self._indicator_post_merge(result)\\n1149: \\n1150:         self._maybe_add_join_keys(result, left_indexer, right_indexer)\\n1151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pandas-dev/pandas@5b19efbb472e64f9fa5a66b5779fcbe0bad3670a/-/blob/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py%3FL1000-1150\"}"

Series]\\\"; expected\\n1262:                 # \\\"Hashable\\\"\\n1263:                 and self.orig_right._is_level_reference(\\n1264:                     right_key  # type: "{\"content\":\"650:         result, _ = _groupby_and_merge(\\n651:             right_by, right, left, lambda x, y: _merger(y, x)\\n652:         )\\n653:     else:\\n654:         result = _merger(left, right)\\n655:     return result\\n656: \\n657: \\n658: @set_module(\\\"pandas\\\")\\n659: def merge_asof(\\n660:     left: DataFrame | Series,\\n661:     right: DataFrame | Series,\\n662:     on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n663:     left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n664:     right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\\n665:     left_index: bool = False,\\n666:     right_index: bool = False,\\n667:     by=None,\\n668:     left_by=None,\\n669:     right_by=None,\\n670:     suffixes: Suffixes = (\\\"_x\\\", \\\"_y\\\"),\\n671:     tolerance: int | datetime.timedelta | None = None,\\n672:     allow_exact_matches: bool = True,\\n673:     direction: str = \\\"backward\\\",\\n674: ) -> DataFrame:\\n675:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n676:     Perform a merge by key distance.\\n677: \\n678:     This is similar to a left-join except that we match on nearest\\n679:     key rather than equal keys. Both DataFrames must be first sorted by\\n680:     the merge key in ascending order before calling this function.\\n681:     Sorting by any additional 'by' grouping columns is not required.\\n682: \\n683:     For each row in the left DataFrame:\\n684: \\n685:       - A \\\"backward\\\" search selects the last row in the right DataFrame whose\\n686:         'on' key is less than or equal to the left's key.\\n687: \\n688:       - A \\\"forward\\\" search selects the first row in the right DataFrame whose\\n689:         'on' key is greater than or equal to the left's key.\\n690: \\n691:       - A \\\"nearest\\\" search selects the row in the right DataFrame whose 'on'\\n692:         key is closest in absolute distance to the left's key.\\n693: \\n694:     Optionally match on equivalent keys with 'by' before searching with 'on'.\\n695: \\n696:     Parameters\\n697:     ----------\\n698:     left : DataFrame or named Series\\n699:         First pandas object to merge.\\n700:     right : DataFrame or named Series\\n701:         Second pandas object to merge.\\n702:     on : label\\n703:         Field name to join on. Must be found in both DataFrames.\\n704:         The data MUST be in ascending order. Furthermore this must be\\n705:         a numeric column, such as datetimelike, integer, or float. ``on``\\n706:         or ``left_on`` / ``right_on`` must be given.\\n707:     left_on : label\\n708:         Field name to join on in left DataFrame. If specified, sort the left\\n709:         DataFrame by this column in ascending order before merging.\\n710:     right_on : label\\n711:         Field name to join on in right DataFrame. If specified, sort the right\\n712:         DataFrame by this column in ascending order before merging.\\n713:     left_index : bool\\n714:         Use the index of the left DataFrame as the join key.\\n715:     right_index : bool\\n716:         Use the index of the right DataFrame as the join key.\\n717:     by : column name or list of column names\\n718:         Match on these columns before performing merge operation. It is not required\\n719:         to sort by these columns.\\n720:     left_by : column name\\n721: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pandas-dev/pandas@5b19efbb472e64f9fa5a66b5779fcbe0bad3670a/-/blob/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py%3FL650-720\"}"

"{\"content\":\"2400:             else:\\n2401:                 lo_dtype = (\\n2402:                     self.left._get_label_or_level_values(left_on_0).dtype\\n2403:                     if left_on_0 in self.left.columns\\n2404:                     else self.left.index.get_level_values(left_on_0)\\n2405:                 )\\n2406:         else:\\n2407:             lo_dtype = self.left.index.dtype\\n2408: \\n2409:         if not self.right_index:\\n2410:             right_on_0 = right_on[0]\\n2411:             if isinstance(right_on_0, _known):\\n2412:                 ro_dtype = right_on_0.dtype\\n2413:             else:\\n2414:                 ro_dtype = (\\n2415:                     self.right._get_label_or_level_values(right_on_0).dtype\\n2416:                     if right_on_0 in self.right.columns\\n2417:                     else self.right.index.get_level_values(right_on_0)\\n2418:                 )\\n2419:         else:\\n2420:             ro_dtype = self.right.index.dtype\\n2421: \\n2422:         if (\\n2423:             is_object_dtype(lo_dtype)\\n2424:             or is_object_dtype(ro_dtype)\\n2425:             or is_string_dtype(lo_dtype)\\n2426:             or is_string_dtype(ro_dtype)\\n2427:         ):\\n2428:             raise MergeError(\\n2429:                 f\\\"Incompatible merge dtype, {lo_dtype!r} and \\\"\\n2430:                 f\\\"{ro_dtype!r}, both sides must have numeric dtype\\\"\\n2431:             )\\n2432: \\n2433:         # add 'by' to our key-list so we can have it in the\\n2434:         # output as a key\\n2435:         if self.left_by is not None:\\n2436:             if not is_list_like(self.left_by):\\n2437:                 self.left_by = [self.left_by]\\n2438:             if not is_list_like(self.right_by):\\n2439:                 self.right_by = [self.right_by]\\n2440: \\n2441:             if len(self.left_by) != len(self.right_by):\\n2442:                 raise MergeError(\\\"left_by and right_by must be the same length\\\")\\n2443: \\n2444:             left_on = self.left_by + list(left_on)\\n2445:             right_on = self.right_by + list(right_on)\\n2446: \\n2447:         return left_on, right_on\\n2448: \\n2449:     def _maybe_require_matching_dtypes(\\n2450:         self, left_join_keys: list[ArrayLike], right_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]\\n2451:     ) -> None:\\n2452:         # TODO: why do we do this for AsOfMerge but not the others?\\n2453: \\n2454:         def _check_dtype_match(left: ArrayLike, right: ArrayLike, i: int) -> None:\\n2455:             if left.dtype != right.dtype:\\n2456:                 if isinstance(left.dtype, CategoricalDtype) and isinstance(\\n2457:                     right.dtype, CategoricalDtype\\n2458:                 ):\\n2459:                     # The generic error message is confusing for categoricals.\\n2460:                     #\\n2461:                     # In this function, the join keys include both the original\\n2462:                     # ones of the merge_asof() call, and also the keys passed\\n2463:                     # to its by= argument. Unordered but equal categories\\n2464:                     # are not supported for the former, but will fail\\n2465:                     # later with a ValueError, so we don't *need* to check\\n2466:                     # for them here.\\n2467:                     msg = (\\n2468:                         f\\\"incompatible merge keys [{i}] {left.dtype!r} and \\\"\\n2469:                         f\\\"{right.dtype!r}, both sides category, but not equal ones\\\"\\n2470:                     )\\n2471:                 else:\\n2472:                     msg = (\\n2473:                         f\\\"incompatible merge keys [{i}] {left.dtype!r} and \\\"\\n2474:                         f\\\"{right.dtype!r}, must be the same type\\\"\\n2475:                     )\\n2476:                 raise MergeError(msg)\\n2477: \\n2478:         # validate index types are the same\\n2479:         for i, (lk, rk) in enumerate(zip(left_join_keys, right_join_keys, strict=True)):\\n2480:             _check_dtype_match(lk, rk, i)\\n2481: \\n2482:         if self.left_index:\\n2483:             lt = self.left.index._values\\n2484:         else:\\n2485:             lt = left_join_keys[-1]\\n2486: \\n2487:         if self.right_index:\\n2488:             rt = self.right.index._values\\n2489:         else:\\n2490:             rt = right_join_keys[-1]\\n2491: \\n2492:         _check_dtype_match(lt, rt, 0)\\n2493: \\n2494:     def _validate_tolerance(self, left_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]) -> None:\\n2495:         # validate tolerance; datetime.timedelta or Timedelta if we have a DTI\\n2496:         if self.tolerance is not None:\\n2497:             if self.left_index:\\n2498:                 lt = self.left.index._values\\n2499:             else:\\n2500:                 lt = left_join_keys[-1]\\n2501: \\n2502:             msg = (\\n2503:                 f\\\"incompatible tolerance {self.tolerance}, must be compat \\\"\\n2504:                 f\\\"with type {lt.dtype!r}\\\"\\n2505:             )\\n2506: \\n2507:             if needs_i8_conversion(lt.dtype) or (\\n2508:                 isinstance(lt, ArrowExtensionArray) and lt.dtype.kind in \\\"mM\\\"\\n2509:             ):\\n2510:                 if not isinstance(self.tolerance, datetime.timedelta):\\n2511:                     raise MergeError(msg)\\n2512:                 if self.tolerance < Timedelta(0):\\n2513:                     raise MergeError(\\\"tolerance must be positive\\\")\\n2514: \\n2515:             elif is_integer_dtype(lt.dtype):\\n2516:                 if not is_integer(self.tolerance):\\n2517:                     raise MergeError(msg)\\n2518:                 if self.tolerance < 0:\\n2519:                     raise MergeError(\\\"tolerance must be positive\\\")\\n2520: \\n2521:             elif is_float_dtype(lt.dtype):\\n2522:                 if not is_number(self.tolerance):\\n2523:                     raise MergeError(msg)\\n2524:                 # error: Unsupported operand types for > (\\\"int\\\" and \\\"Number\\\")\\n2525:                 if self.tolerance < 0:  # type: ignore[operator]\\n2526:                     raise MergeError(\\\"tolerance must be positive\\\")\\n2527: \\n2528:             else:\\n2529:                 raise MergeError(\\\"key must be integer, timestamp or float\\\")\\n2530: \\n2531:     def _convert_values_for_libjoin(\\n2532:         self, values: AnyArrayLike, side: str\\n2533:     ) -> np.ndarray:\\n2534:         # we require sortedness and non-null values in the join keys\\n2535:         if not Index(values, copy=False).is_monotonic_increasing:\\n2536:             if isna(values).any():\\n2537:                 raise ValueError(f\\\"Merge keys contain null values on {side} side\\\")\\n2538:             raise ValueError(f\\\"{side} keys must be sorted\\\")\\n2539: \\n2540:         if isinstance(values, ArrowExtensionArray):\\n2541:             values = values._maybe_convert_datelike_array()\\n2542: \\n2543:         if needs_i8_conversion(values.dtype):\\n2544:             values = values.view(\\\"i8\\\")\\n2545: \\n2546:         elif isinstance(values, BaseMaskedArray):\\n2547:             # we've verified above that no nulls exist\\n2548:             values = values._data\\n2549:         elif isinstance(values, ExtensionArray):\\n2550:             values = values.to_numpy()\\n2551: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pandas-dev/pandas@5b19efbb472e64f9fa5a66b5779fcbe0bad3670a/-/blob/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py%3FL2400-2550\"}"

a \\\"by\\\" parameter requires special handling\\n2598:         if self.left_by is not None:\\n2599:             # remove 'on' parameter from values if one existed\\n2600:             if self.left_index and self.right_index:\\n2601:                 left_join_keys = self.left_join_keys\\n2602:                 right_join_keys = self.right_join_keys\\n2603:             else:\\n2604:                 left_join_keys = self.left_join_keys[0:-1]\\n2605:                 right_join_keys = self.right_join_keys[0:-1]\\n2606: \\n2607:             mapped = [\\n2608:                 _factorize_keys(\\n2609:                     left_join_keys[n],\\n2610:                     right_join_keys[n],\\n2611:                     sort=False,\\n2612:                 )\\n2613:                 for n in range(len(left_join_keys))\\n2614:             ]\\n2615: \\n2616:             if len(left_join_keys) == 1:\\n2617:                 left_by_values = mapped[0][0]\\n2618:                 right_by_values = mapped[0][1]\\n2619:             else:\\n2620:                 arrs = [np.concatenate(m[:2]) for m in mapped]\\n2621:                 shape = tuple(m[2] for m in mapped)\\n2622:                 group_index = get_group_index(\\n2623:                     arrs, shape=shape, sort=False, xnull=False\\n2624:                 )\\n2625:                 left_len = len(left_join_keys[0])\\n2626:                 left_by_values = group_index[:left_len]\\n2627:                 right_by_values = group_index[left_len:]\\n2628: \\n2629:             left_by_values = ensure_int64(left_by_values)\\n2630:             right_by_values = ensure_int64(right_by_values)\\n2631: \\n2632:             # choose appropriate function by type\\n2633:             func = _asof_by_function(self.direction)\\n2634:             return func(\\n2635:                 left_values,\\n2636:                 right_values,\\n2637:                 left_by_values,\\n2638:                 right_by_values,\\n2639:                 self.allow_exact_matches,\\n2640:                 tolerance,\\n2641:             )\\n2642:         else:\\n2643:             # choose appropriate function by type\\n2644:             func = _asof_by_function(self.direction)\\n2645:             return func(\\n2646:                 left_values,\\n2647:                 right_values,\\n2648:                 None,\\n2649:                 None,\\n2650:                 self.allow_exact_matches,\\n2651:                 tolerance,\\n2652:                 False,\\n2653:             )\\n2654: \\n2655: \\n2656: def _get_multiindex_indexer(\\n2657:     join_keys: list[ArrayLike], index: MultiIndex, sort: bool\\n2658: ) -> tuple[npt.NDArray[np.intp], npt.NDArray[np.intp]]:\\n2659:     # left & right join labels and num. of levels at each location\\n2660:     mapped = (\\n2661:         _factorize_keys(index.levels[n]._values, join_keys[n], sort=sort)\\n2662:         for n in range(index.nlevels)\\n2663:     )\\n2664:     zipped = zip(*mapped, strict=True)\\n2665:     rcodes, lcodes, shape = (list(x) for x in zipped)\\n2666:     if sort:\\n2667:         rcodes = list(map(np.take, rcodes, index.codes))\\n2668:     else:\\n2669:         i8copy = lambda a: a.astype(\\\"i8\\\", subok=False)\\n2670:         rcodes = list(map(i8copy, index.codes))\\n2671: \\n2672:     # fix right labels if there were any nulls\\n2673:     for i, join_key in enumerate(join_keys):\\n2674:         mask = index.codes[i] == -1\\n2675:         if mask.any():\\n2676:             # check if there already was any nulls at this location\\n2677:             # if there was, it is factorized to `shape[i] - 1`\\n2678:             a = join_key[lcodes[i] == shape[i] - 1]\\n2679:             if a.size == 0 or not a[0] != a[0]:\\n2680:                 shape[i] += 1\\n2681: \\n2682:             rcodes[i][mask] = shape[i] - 1\\n2683: \\n2684:     # get flat i8 join keys\\n2685:     lkey, rkey = _get_join_keys(lcodes, rcodes, tuple(shape), sort)\\n2686:     return lkey, rkey\\n2687: \\n2688: \\n2689: def _get_empty_indexer() -> tuple[npt.NDArray[np.intp], npt.NDArray[np.intp]]:\\n2690:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Return empty join indexers.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2691:     return (\\n2692:         np.array([], dtype=np.intp),\\n2693:         np.array([], dtype=np.intp),\\n2694:     )\\n2695: \\n2696: \\n2697: def _get_no_sort_one_missing_indexer(\\n2698:     n: int, left_missing: bool\\n2699: ) -> tuple[npt.NDArray[np.intp], npt.NDArray[np.intp]]:\\n2700:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2701:     Return join indexers where all of one side is selected without sorting\\n2702:     and none of the other side is selected.\\n2703: \\n2704:     Parameters\\n2705:     ----------\\n2706:     n : int\\n2707:         Length of indexers to create.\\n2708:     left_missing : bool\\n2709:         If True, the left indexer will contain only -1's.\\n2710:         If False, the right indexer will contain only -1's.\\n2711: \\n2712:     Returns\\n2713:     -------\\n2714:     np.ndarray[np.intp]\\n2715:         Left indexer\\n2716:     np.ndarray[np.intp]\\n2717:         Right indexer\\n2718:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2719:     idx = np.arange(n, dtype=np.intp)\\n2720:     idx_missing = np.full(shape=n, fill_value=-1, dtype=np.intp)\\n2721:     if left_missing:\\n2722:         return idx_missing, idx\\n2723:     return idx, idx_missing\\n2724: \\n2725: \\n2726: def _left_join_on_index(\\n2727:     left_ax: Index, right_ax: Index, join_keys: list[ArrayLike], sort: bool = False\\n2728: ) -> tuple[Index, npt.NDArray[np.intp] | None, npt.NDArray[np.intp]]:\\n2729:     if isinstance(right_ax, MultiIndex):\\n2730:         lkey, rkey = _get_multiindex_indexer(join_keys, right_ax, sort=sort)\\n2731:     else:\\n2732:         # error: Incompatible types in assignment (expression has type\\n2733:         # \\\"Union[Union[ExtensionArray, ndarray[Any, Any]], Index, Series]\\\",\\n2734:         # variable has type \\\"ndarray[Any, dtype[signedinteger[Any]]]\\\")\\n2735:         lkey = join_keys[0]  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2736:         # error: Incompatible types in assignment (expression has type \\\"Index\\\",\\n2737:         # variable has type \\\"ndarray[Any, dtype[signedinteger[Any]]]\\\")\\n2738:         rkey = right_ax._values  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2739: \\n2740:     left_key, right_key, count = _factorize_keys(lkey, rkey, sort=sort)\\n2741:     left_indexer, right_indexer = libjoin.left_outer_join(\\n2742:         left_key, right_key, count, sort=sort\\n2743:     )\\n2744: \\n2745:     if sort or len(left_ax) != len(left_indexer):\\n2746:         # if asked to sort or there are 1-to-many matches\\n2747:         join_index = left_ax.take(left_indexer)\\n2748:         return join_index, left_indexer, right_indexer\\n2749: \\n2750:     # left frame preserves order & length of its index\\n2751: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pandas-dev/pandas@5b19efbb472e64f9fa5a66b5779fcbe0bad3670a/-/blob/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py%3FL2550-2750\"}"

pandas.core.dtypes.missing import (\n74:     isna,\n75:     na_value_for_dtype,\n76: )\n77: \n78: from pandas import (\n79:     ArrowDtype,\n80:     Categorical,\n81:     Index,\n82: ExtensionArray,\n90: )\n91: from pandas.core.arrays.string_ import StringDtype\n92: import pandas.core.common as com\n93: from pandas.core.construction import (\n94:     ensure_wrapped_if_datetimelike,\n95:     extract_array,\n96: )\n97: from pandas.core.frame import _merge_doc\n98: from pandas.core.indexes.api import default_index\n99: from pandas.core.sorting import (\n100:     get_group_index,\n101:     is_int64_overflow_possible,\n102: )\n103: \n104: if TYPE_CHECKING:\n105:     from pandas import DataFrame\n106:     from pandas.core import groupby\n107:     from pandas.core.arrays import DatetimeArray\n108:     from pandas.core.indexes.frozen import FrozenList\n109: \n110: _factorizers = {\n111:     np.int64: libhashtable.Int64Factorizer,\n112:     np.longlong: libhashtable.Int64Factorizer,\n113:     np.int32: libhashtable.Int32Factorizer,\n114:     np.int16: libhashtable.Int16Factorizer,\n115:     np.int8: libhashtable.Int8Factorizer,\n116:     np.uint64: libhashtable.UInt64Factorizer,\n117:     np.uint32: libhashtable.UInt32Factorizer,\n118:     np.uint16: libhashtable.UInt16Factorizer,\n119:     np.uint8: libhashtable.UInt8Factorizer,\n120:     np.bool_: libhashtable.UInt8Factorizer,\n121:     np.float64: libhashtable.Float64Factorizer,\n122:     np.float32: libhashtable.Float32Factorizer,\n123:     np.complex64: libhashtable.Complex64Factorizer,\n124:     np.complex128: libhashtable.Complex128Factorizer,\n125:     np.object_: libhashtable.ObjectFactorizer,\n126: }\n127: \n128: # See https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/52451\n129: if np.intc is not np.int32:\n130:     _factorizers[np.intc] = libhashtable.Int64Factorizer\n131: \n132: _known = (np.ndarray, ExtensionArray, Index, ABCSeries)\n133: \n134: \n135: @Substitution(\"\\nleft : DataFrame or named Series\")\n136: @Appender(_merge_doc, indents=0)\n137: def merge(\n138:     left: DataFrame | Series,\n139:     right: DataFrame | Series,\n140:     how: MergeHow = \"inner\",\n141:     on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n142:     left_on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n143:     right_on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n144:     left_index: bool = False,\n145:     right_index: bool = False,\n146:     sort: bool = False,\n147:     suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n148:     copy: bool | None = None,\n149:     indicator: str | bool = False,\n150:     validate: str | None = None,\n151: ) -> DataFrame:\n152:     left_df = _validate_operand(left)\n153:     right_df = _validate_operand(right)\n154:     if how == \"cross\":\n155:         return _cross_merge(\n156:             left_df,\n157:             right_df,\n158:             on=on,\n159:             left_on=left_on,\n160:             right_on=right_on,\n161:             left_index=left_index,\n162:             right_index=right_index,\n163:             sort=sort,\n164:             suffixes=suffixes,\n165:             indicator=indicator,\n166:             validate=validate,\n167:             copy=copy,\n168:         )\n169:     else:\n170:         op = _MergeOperation(\n171:             left_df,\n172:             right_df,\n173:             how=how,\n174:             on=on,\n175:             left_on=left_on,\n176:             right_on=right_on,\n177:             left_index=left_index,\n178:             right_index=right_index,\n179:             sort=sort,\n180:             suffixes=suffixes,\n181:             indicator=indicator,\n182:             validate=validate,\n183:         )\n184:         return op.get_result(copy=copy)\n185: \n186: \n187: def _cross_merge(\n188:     left: DataFrame,\n189:     right: DataFrame,\n190:     on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n191:     left_on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n192:     right_on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n193:     left_index: bool = False,\n194:     right_index: bool = False,\n195:     sort: bool = False,\n196:     suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n197:     copy: bool | None = None,\n198:     indicator: str | bool = False,\n199:     validate: str | None = None,\n200: ) -> DataFrame:\n201:     \"\"\"\n202:     See merge.__doc__ with how='cross'\n203:     \"\"\"\n204: \n205:     if (\n206:         left_index\n207:         or right_index\n208:         or right_on is not None\n209:         or left_on is not None\n210:         or on is not None\n211:     ):\n212:         raise MergeError(\n213:             \"Can not pass on, right_on, left_on or set right_index=True or \"\n214:             \"left_index=True\"\n215:         )\n216: \n217:     cross_col = f\"_cross_{uuid.uuid4()}\"\n218:     left = left.assign(**{cross_col: 1})\n219:     right = right.assign(**{cross_col: 1})\n220: \n221:     left_on = right_on = [cross_col]\n222: \n223:     res = merge(\n224:         left,\n225:         right,\n226:         how=\"inner\",\n227:         on=on,\n228:         left_on=left_on,\n229:         right_on=right_on,\n230:         left_index=left_index,\n231:         right_index=right_index,\n232:         sort=sort,\n233:         suffixes=suffixes,\n234:         indicator=indicator,\n235:         validate=validate,\n236:         copy=copy,\n237:     )\n238:     del res[cross_col]\n239:     return res\n240: \n241: \n242: def _groupby_and_merge(\n243:     by, left: DataFrame | Series, right: DataFrame | Series, merge_pieces\n244: ):\n245:     \"\"\"\n246:     groupby & merge; we are always performing a left-by type operation\n247: \n248:     Parameters\n249:     ----------\n250:     by: field to group\n251:     left: DataFrame\n252:     right: DataFrame\n253:     merge_pieces: function for merging\n254:     \"\"\"\n255:     pieces = []\n256:     if not isinstance(by, (list, tuple)):\n257:         by = [by]\n258: \n259:     lby = left.groupby(by, sort=False)\n260:     rby: groupby.DataFrameGroupBy | groupby.SeriesGroupBy | None = None\n261: \n262:     # if we can groupby the rhs\n263:     # then we can get vastly better perf\n264:     if all(item in right.columns for item in by):\n265:         rby = right.groupby(by, sort=False)\n266: \n267:     for key, lhs in lby._grouper.get_iterator(lby._selected_obj, axis=lby.axis):\n268:         if rby is None:\n269:             rhs = right\n270:         else:\n271:             try:\n272:                 rhs = right.take(rby.indices[key])\n273:             except KeyError:\n274:                 # key doesn't exist in left\n275:                 lcols = lhs.columns.tolist()\n276:                 cols = lcols + [r for r in right.columns if r not in set(lcols)]\n277:                 merged = lhs.reindex(columns=cols)\n278:                 merged.index = range(len(merged))\n279:                 pieces.append(merged)\n280:                 continue\n281: \n282:         merged = merge_pieces(lhs, rhs)\n283: \n284:         # make sure join keys are in the merged\n285:         # TODO, should merge_pieces do this?\n286:         merged[by] = key\n287: \n288:         pieces.append(merged)\n289: \n290:     # preserve the original order\n291:     # if we have a missing piece this can be reset\n292:     from pandas.core.reshape.concat import concat\n293: \n294:     result = concat(pieces, ignore_index=True)\n295:     result = result.reindex(columns=pieces[0].columns, copy=False)\n296:     return result, lby\n297: \n298: \n299: def merge_ordered(\n300:     left: DataFrame | Series,\n301:     right: DataFrame | Series,\n302:     on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n303:     left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n304:     right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n305:     left_by=None,\n306:     right_by=None,\n307:     fill_method: str | None = None,\n308:     suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n309:     how: JoinHow = \"outer\",\n310: ) -> DataFrame:\n311:     \"\"\"\n312:     Perform a merge for ordered data with optional filling/interpolation.\n313: \n314:     Designed for ordered data like time series data. Optionally\n315:     perform group-wise merge (see examples).\n316: \n317:     Parameters\n318:     ----------\n319:     left : DataFrame or named Series\n320:     right : DataFrame or named Series\n321:     on : label or list\n322:         Field names to join on. Must be found in both DataFrames.\n323:     left_on : label or list, or array-like\n324:         Field names to join on in left DataFrame. Can be a vector or list of\n325:         vectors of the length of the DataFrame to use a particular vector as\n326:         the join key instead of columns.\n327:     right_on : label or list, or array-like\n328:         Field names to join on in right DataFrame or vector/list of vectors per\n329:         left_on docs.\n330:     left_by : column name or list of column names\n331:         Group left DataFrame by group columns and merge piece by piece with\n332:         right DataFrame. Must be None if either left or right are a Series.\n333:     right_by : column name or list of column names\n334:         Group right DataFrame by group columns and merge piece by piece with\n335:         left DataFrame. Must be None if either left or right are a Series.\n336:     fill_method : {'ffill', None}, default None\n337:         Interpolation method for data.\n338:     suffixes : list-like, default is (\"_x\", \"_y\")\n339:         A length-2 sequence where each element is optionally a string\n340:         indicating the suffix to add to overlapping column names in\n341:         `left` and `right` respectively. Pass a value of `None` instead\n342:         of a string to indicate that the column name from `left` or\n343:         `right` should be left as-is, with no suffix. At least one of the\n344:         values must not be None.\n345: \n346:     how : {'left', 'right', 'outer', 'inner'}, default 'outer'\n347:         * left: use only keys from left frame (SQL: left outer join)\n348:         * right: use only keys from right frame (SQL: right outer join)\n349:         * outer: use union of keys from both frames (SQL: full outer join)\n350:         * inner: use intersection of keys from both frames (SQL: inner join).\n351: \n352:     Returns\n353:     -------\n354:     DataFrame\n355:         The merged DataFrame output type will be the same as\n356:         'left', if it is a subclass of DataFrame.\n357: \n358:     See Also\n359:     --------\n360:     merge : Merge with a database-style join.\n361:     merge_asof : Merge on nearest keys.\n362: \n363:     Examples\n364:     --------\n365:     >>> from pandas import merge_ordered\n366:     >>> df1 = pd.DataFrame(\n367:     ...     {\n368:     ...         \"key\": [\"a\", \"c\", \"e\", \"a\", \"c\", \"e\"],\n369:     ...         \"lvalue\": [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3],\n370:     ...         \"group\": [\"a\", \"a\", \"a\", \"b\", \"b\", \"b\"]\n371:     ...     }\n372:     ... )\n373:     >>> df1\n374:       key  lvalue group\n375:     0   a       1     a\n376:     1   c       2     a\n377:     2   e       3     a\n378:     3   a       1     b\n379:     4   c       2     b\n380:     5   e       3     b\n381: \n382:     >>> df2 = pd.DataFrame({\"key\": [\"b\", \"c\", \"d\"], \"rvalue\": [1, 2, 3]})\n383:     >>> df2\n384:       key  rvalue\n385:     0   b       1\n386:     1   c       2\n387:     2   d       3\n388: \n389:     >>> merge_ordered(df1, df2, fill_method=\"ffill\", left_by=\"group\")\n390:       key  lvalue group  rvalue\n391:     0   a       1     a     NaN\n392:     1   b       1     a     1.0\n393:     2   c       2     a     2.0\n394:     3   d       2     a     3.0\n395:     4   e       3     a     3.0\n396:     5   a       1     b     NaN\n397:     6   b       1     b     1.0\n398:     7   c       2     b     2.0\n399:     8   d       2     b     3.0\n400:     9   e       3     b     3.0\n401:     \"\"\"\n402: \n403:     def _merger(x, y) -> DataFrame:\n404:         # perform the ordered merge operation\n405:         op = _OrderedMerge(\n406:             x,\n407:             y,\n408:             on=on,\n409:             left_on=left_on,\n410:             right_on=right_on,\n411:             suffixes=suffixes,\n412:             fill_method=fill_method,\n413:             how=how,\n414:         )\n415:         return op.get_result()\n416: \n417:     if left_by is not None and right_by is not None:\n418:         raise ValueError(\"Can only group either left or right frames\")\n419:     if left_by is not None:\n420:         if isinstance(left_by, str):\n421:             left_by = [left_by]\n422:         check = set(left_by).difference(left.columns)\n423:         if len(check) != 0:\n424:             raise KeyError(f\"{check} not found in left columns\")\n425:         result, _ = _groupby_and_merge(left_by, left, right, lambda x, y: _merger(x, y))\n426:     elif right_by is not None:\n427:         if isinstance(right_by, str):\n428:             right_by = [right_by]\n429:         check = set(right_by).difference(right.columns)\n430:         if len(check) != 0:\n431:             raise KeyError(f\"{check} not found in right columns\")\n432:         result, _ = _groupby_and_merge(\n433:             right_by, right, left, lambda x, y: _merger(y, x)\n434:         )\n435:     else:\n436:         result = _merger(left, right)\n437:     return result\n438: \n439: \n440: def merge_asof(\n441:     left: DataFrame | Series,\n442:     right: DataFrame | Series,\n443:     on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n444:     left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n445:     right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,\n446:     left_index: bool = False,\n447:     right_index: bool = False,\n448:     by=None,\n449:     left_by=None,\n450:     right_by=None,\n451:     suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n452:     tolerance: int | Timedelta | None = None,\n453:     allow_exact_matches: bool = True,\n454:     direction: str = \"backward\",\n455: ) -> DataFrame:\n456:     \"\"\"\n457:     Perform a merge by key distance.\n458: \n459:     This is similar to a left-join except that we match on nearest\n460:     key rather than equal keys. Both DataFrames must be sorted by the key.\n461: \n462:     For each row in the left DataFrame:\n463: \n464:       - A \"backward\" search selects the last row in the right DataFrame whose\n465:         'on' key is less than or equal to the left's key.\n466: \n467:       - A \"forward\" search selects the first row in the right DataFrame whose\n468:         'on' key is greater than or equal to the left's key.\n469: \n470:       - A \"nearest\" search selects the row in the right DataFrame whose 'on'\n471:         key is closest in absolute distance to the left's key.\n472: \n473:     Optionally match on equivalent keys with 'by' before searching with 'on'.\n474: \n475:     Parameters\n476:     ----------\n477:     left : DataFrame or named Series\n478:     right : DataFrame or named Series\n479:     on : label\n480:         Field name to join on. Must be found in both DataFrames.\n481:         The data MUST be ordered. Furthermore this must be a numeric column,\n482:         such as datetimelike, integer, or float. On or left_on/right_on\n483:         must be given.\n484:     left_on : label\n485:         Field name to join on in left DataFrame.\n486:     right_on : label\n487:         Field name to join on in right DataFrame.\n488:     left_index : bool\n489:         Use the index of the left DataFrame as the join key.\n490:     right_index : bool\n491:         Use the index of the right DataFrame as the join key.\n492:     by : column name or list of column names\n493:         Match on these columns before performing merge operation.\n494:     left_by : column name\n495:         Field names to match on in the left DataFrame.\n496:     right_by : column name\n497:         Field names to match on in the right DataFrame.\n498:     suffixes : 2-length sequence (tuple, list, ...)\n499:         Suffix to apply to overlapping column names in the left and right\n500:         side, respectively.\n501:     tolerance : int or Timedelta, optional, default None\n502:         Select asof tolerance within this range; must be compatible\n503:         with the merge index.\n504:     allow_exact_matches : bool, default True\n505: \n506:         - If True, allow matching with the same 'on' value\n507:           (i.e. less-than-or-equal-to / greater-than-or-equal-to)\n508:         - If False, don't match the same 'on' value\n509:           (i.e., strictly less-than / strictly greater-than).\n510: \n511:     direction : 'backward' (default), 'forward', or 'nearest'\n512:         Whether to search for prior, subsequent, or closest matches.\n513: \n514:     Returns\n515:     -------\n516:     DataFrame\n517: \n518:     See Also\n519:     --------\n520:     merge : Merge with a database-style join.\n521:     merge_ordered : Merge with optional filling/interpolation.\n522: \n523:     Examples\n524:     --------\n525:     >>> left = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 5, 10], \"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]})\n526:     >>> left\n527:         a left_val\n528:     0   1        a\n529:     1   5        b\n530:     2  10        c\n531: \n532:     >>> right = pd.DataFrame({\"a\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], \"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})\n533:     >>> right\n534:        a  right_val\n535:     0  1          1\n536:     1  2          2\n537:     2  3          3\n538:     3  6          6\n539:     4  7          7\n540: \n541:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\")\n542:         a left_val  right_val\n543:     0   1        a          1\n544:     1   5        b          3\n545:     2  10        c          7\n546: \n547:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", allow_exact_matches=False)\n548:         a left_val  right_val\n549:     0   1        a        NaN\n550:     1   5        b        3.0\n551:     2  10        c        7.0\n552: \n553:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"forward\")\n554:         a left_val  right_val\n555:     0   1        a        1.0\n556:     1   5        b        6.0\n557:     2  10        c        NaN\n558: \n559:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, on=\"a\", direction=\"nearest\")\n560:         a left_val  right_val\n561:     0   1        a          1\n562:     1   5        b          6\n563:     2  10        c          7\n564: \n565:     We can use indexed DataFrames as well.\n566: \n567:     >>> left = pd.DataFrame({\"left_val\": [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"]}, index=[1, 5, 10])\n568:     >>> left\n569:        left_val\n570:     1         a\n571:     5         b\n572:     10        c\n573: \n574:     >>> right = pd.DataFrame({\"right_val\": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]}, index=[1, 2, 3, 6, 7])\n575:     >>> right\n576:        right_val\n577:     1          1\n578:     2          2\n579:     3          3\n580:     6          6\n581:     7          7\n582: \n583:     >>> pd.merge_asof(left, right, left_index=True, right_index=True)\n584:        left_val  right_val\n585:     1         a          1\n586:     5         b          3\n587:     10        c          7\n588: \n589:     Here is a real-world times-series example\n590: \n591:     >>> quotes = pd.DataFrame(\n592:     ...     {\n593:     ...         \"time\": [\n594:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.023\"),\n595:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.023\"),\n596:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.030\"),\n597:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.041\"),\n598:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.048\"),\n599:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.049\"),\n600:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.072\"),\n601:     ...             pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.075\")\n602:     ...         ],\n603:     ...         \"ticker\": [\n604:     ...                \"GOOG\",\n605:     ...                \"MSFT\",\n606:     ...                \"MSFT\",\n607:     ...                \"MSFT\",\n608:     ...                \"GOOG\",\n609:     ...                \"AAPL\",\n610:     ...                \"GOOG\",\n611:     ...                \"MSFT\"\n612:     ...            ],\n613:     ...            \"bid\": [720.50, 51.95, 51.97, 51.99, 720.50, 97.99, 720.50, 52.01],\n614:     ...            \"ask\": [720.93, 51.96, 51.98, 52.00, 720.93, 98.01, 720.88, 52.03]\n615:     ...     }\n616:     ... )\n617:     >>> quotes\n618:                          time ticker     bid     ask\n619:     0 2016-05-25 13:30:00.023   GOOG  720.50  720.93\n620:     1 2016-05-25 13:30:00.023   MSFT   51.95   51.96\n621:     2 2016-05-25 13:30:00.030   MSFT   51.97   51.98\n622:     3 2016-05-25 13:30:00.041   MSFT   51.99   52.00\n623:     4 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.50  720.93\n624:     5 2016-05-25 13:30:00.049   AAPL   97.99   98.01\n625:     6 2016-05-25 13:30:00.072   GOOG  720.50  720.88\n626:     7 2016-05-25 13:30:00.075   MSFT   52.01   52.03\n627: \n628:     >>> trades = pd.DataFrame(\n629:     ...        {\n630:     ...            \"time\": [\n631:     ...                pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.023\"),\n632:     ...                pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.038\"),\n633:     ...                pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.048\"),\n634:     ...                pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.048\"),\n635:     ...                pd.Timestamp(\"2016-05-25 13:30:00.048\")\n636:     ...            ],\n637:     ...            \"ticker\": [\"MSFT\", \"MSFT\", \"GOOG\", \"GOOG\", \"AAPL\"],\n638:     ...            \"price\": [51.95, 51.95, 720.77, 720.92, 98.0],\n639:     ...            \"quantity\": [75, 155, 100, 100, 100]\n640:     ...        }\n641:     ...    )\n642:     >>> trades\n643:                          time ticker   price  quantity\n644:     0 2016-05-25 13:30:00.023   MSFT   51.95        75\n645:     1 2016-05-25 13:30:00.038   MSFT   51.95       155\n646:     2 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.77       100\n647:     3 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.92       100\n648:     4 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   AAPL   98.00       100\n649: \n650:     By default we are taking the asof of the quotes\n651: \n652:     >>> pd.merge_asof(trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\")\n653:                          time ticker   price  quantity     bid     ask\n654:     0 2016-05-25 13:30:00.023   MSFT   51.95        75   51.95   51.96\n655:     1 2016-05-25 13:30:00.038   MSFT   51.95       155   51.97   51.98\n656:     2 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.77       100  720.50  720.93\n657:     3 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.92       100  720.50  720.93\n658:     4 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   AAPL   98.00       100     NaN     NaN\n659: \n660:     We only asof within 2ms between the quote time and the trade time\n661: \n662:     >>> pd.merge_asof(\n663:     ...     trades, quotes, on=\"time\", by=\"ticker\", tolerance=pd.Timedelta(\"2ms\")\n664:     ... )\n665:                          time ticker   price  quantity     bid     ask\n666:     0 2016-05-25 13:30:00.023   MSFT   51.95        75   51.95   51.96\n667:     1 2016-05-25 13:30:00.038   MSFT   51.95       155     NaN     NaN\n668:     2 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.77       100  720.50  720.93\n669:     3 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.92       100  720.50  720.93\n670:     4 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   AAPL   98.00       100     NaN     NaN\n671: \n672:     We only asof within 10ms between the quote time and the trade time\n673:     and we exclude exact matches on time. However *prior* data will\n674:     propagate forward\n675: \n676:     >>> pd.merge_asof(\n677:     ...     trades,\n678:     ...     quotes,\n679:     ...     on=\"time\",\n680:     ...     by=\"ticker\",\n681:     ...     tolerance=pd.Timedelta(\"10ms\"),\n682:     ...     allow_exact_matches=False\n683:     ... )\n684:                          time ticker   price  quantity     bid     ask\n685:     0 2016-05-25 13:30:00.023   MSFT   51.95        75     NaN     NaN\n686:     1 2016-05-25 13:30:00.038   MSFT   51.95       155   51.97   51.98\n687:     2 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.77       100     NaN     NaN\n688:     3 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   GOOG  720.92       100     NaN     NaN\n689:     4 2016-05-25 13:30:00.048   AAPL   98.00       100     NaN     NaN\n690:     \"\"\"\n691:     op = _AsOfMerge(\n692:         left,\n693:         right,\n694:         on=on,\n695:         left_on=left_on,\n696:         right_on=right_on,\n697:         left_index=left_index,\n698:         right_index=right_index,\n699:         by=by,\n700:         left_by=left_by,\n701:         right_by=right_by,\n702:         suffixes=suffixes,\n703:         how=\"asof\",\n704:         tolerance=tolerance,\n705:         allow_exact_matches=allow_exact_matches,\n706:         direction=direction,\n707:     )\n708:     return op.get_result()\n709: \n710: \n711: # TODO: transformations??\n712: # TODO: only copy DataFrames when modification necessary\n713: class _MergeOperation:\n714:     \"\"\"\n715:     Perform a database (SQL) merge operation between two DataFrame or Series\n716:     objects using either columns as keys or their row indexes\n717:     \"\"\"\n718: \n719:     _merge_type = \"merge\"\n720:     how: JoinHow | Literal[\"asof\"]\n721:     on: IndexLabel | None\n722:     # left_on/right_on may be None when passed, but in validate_specification\n723:     #  get replaced with non-None.\n724:     left_on: Sequence[Hashable | AnyArrayLike]\n725:     right_on: Sequence[Hashable | AnyArrayLike]\n726:     left_index: bool\n727:     right_index: bool\n728:     sort: bool\n729:     suffixes: Suffixes\n730:     copy: bool\n731:     indicator: str | bool\n732:     validate: str | None\n733:     join_names: list[Hashable]\n734:     right_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]\n735:     left_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]\n736: \n737:     def __init__(\n738:         self,\n739:         left: DataFrame | Series,\n740:         right: DataFrame | Series,\n741:         how: JoinHow | Literal[\"asof\"] = \"inner\",\n742:         on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n743:         left_on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n744:         right_on: IndexLabel | AnyArrayLike | None = None,\n745:         left_index: bool = False,\n746:         right_index: bool = False,\n747:         sort: bool = True,\n748:         suffixes: Suffixes = (\"_x\", \"_y\"),\n749:         indicator: str | bool = False,\n750:         validate: str | None = None,\n751:     ) -> None:\n752:         _left = _validate_operand(left)\n753:         _right = _validate_operand(right)\n754:         self.left = self.orig_left = _left\n755:         self.right = self.orig_right = _right\n756:         self.how = how\n757: \n758:         self.on = com.maybe_make_list(on)\n759: \n760:         self.suffixes = suffixes\n761:         self.sort = sort or how == \"outer\"\n762: \n763:         self.left_index = left_index\n764:         self.right_index = right_index\n765: \n766:         self.indicator = indicator\n767: \n768:         if not is_bool(left_index):\n769:             raise ValueError(\n770:                 f\"left_index parameter must be of type bool, not {type(left_index)}\"\n771:             )\n772:         if not is_bool(right_index):\n773:             raise ValueError(\n774:                 f\"right_index parameter must be of type bool, not {type(right_index)}\"\n775:             )\n776: \n777:         # GH 40993: raise when merging between different levels; enforced in 2.0\n778:         if _left.columns.nlevels != _right.columns.nlevels:\n779:             msg = (\n780:                 \"Not allowed to merge between different levels. \"\n781:                 f\"({_left.columns.nlevels} levels on the left, \"\n782:                 f\"{_right.columns.nlevels} on the right)\"\n783:             )\n784:             raise MergeError(msg)\n785: \n786:         self.left_on, self.right_on = self._validate_left_right_on(left_on, right_on)\n787: \n788:         (\n789:             self.left_join_keys,\n790:             self.right_join_keys,\n791:             self.join_names,\n792:             left_drop,\n793:             right_drop,\n794:         ) = self._get_merge_keys()\n795: \n796:         if left_drop:\n797:             self.left = self.left._drop_labels_or_levels(left_drop)\n798: \n799:         if right_drop:\n800:             self.right = self.right._drop_labels_or_levels(right_drop)\n801: \n802:         self._maybe_require_matching_dtypes(self.left_join_keys, self.right_join_keys)\n803:         self._validate_tolerance(self.left_join_keys)\n804: \n805:         # validate the merge keys dtypes. We may need to coerce\n806:         # to avoid incompatible dtypes\n807:         self._maybe_coerce_merge_keys()\n808: \n809:         # If argument passed to validate,\n810:         # check if columns specified as unique\n811:         # are in fact unique.\n812:         if validate is not None:\n813:             self._validate_validate_kwd(validate)\n814: \n815:     def _maybe_require_matching_dtypes(\n816:         self, left_join_keys: list[ArrayLike], right_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]\n817:     ) -> None:\n818:         # Overridden by AsOfMerge\n819:         pass\n820: \n821:     def _validate_tolerance(self, left_join_keys: list[ArrayLike]) -> None:\n822:         # Overridden by AsOfMerge\n823:         pass\n824: \n825:     @final\n826:     def _reindex_and_concat(\n827:         self,\n828:         join_index: Index,\n829:         left_indexer: npt.NDArray[np.intp] | None,\n830:         right_indexer: npt.NDArray[np.intp] | None,\n831:         copy: bool | None,\n832:     ) -> DataFrame:\n833:         \"\"\"\n834:         reindex along index and concat along columns.\n835:         \"\"\"\n836:         # Take views so we do not alter the originals\n837:         left = self.left[:]\n838:         right = self.right[:]\n839: \n840:         llabels, rlabels = _items_overlap_with_suffix(\n841:             self.left._info_axis, self.right._info_axis, self.suffixes\n842:         )\n843: \n844:         if left_indexer is not None and not is_range_indexer(left_indexer, len(left)):\n845:             # Pinning the index here (and in the right code just below) is not\n846:             #  necessary, but makes the `.take` more performant if we have e.g.\n847:             #  a MultiIndex for left.index.\n848:             lmgr = left._mgr.reindex_indexer(\n849:                 join_index,\n850:                 left_indexer,\n851:                 axis=1,\n852:                 copy=False,\n853:                 only_slice=True,\n854:                 allow_dups=True,\n855:                 use_na_proxy=True,\n856:             )\n857:             left = left._constructor_from_mgr(lmgr, axes=lmgr.axes)\n858:         left.index = join_index\n859: \n860:         if right_indexer is not None and not is_range_indexer(\n861:             right_indexer, len(right)\n862:         ):\n863:             rmgr = right._mgr.reindex_indexer(\n864:                 join_index,\n865:                 right_indexer,\n866:                 axis=1,\n867:                 copy=False,\n868:                 only_slice=True,\n869:                 allow_dups=True,\n870:                 use_na_proxy=True,\n871:             )\n872:             right = right._constructor_from_mgr(rmgr, axes=rmgr.axes)\n873:         right.index = join_index\n874: \n875:         from pandas import concat\n876: \n877:         left.columns = llabels\n878:         right.columns = rlabels\n879:         result = concat([left, right], axis=1, copy=copy)\n880:         return result\n881: \n882:     def get_result(self, copy: bool | None = True) -> DataFrame:\n883:         if self.indicator:\n884:             self.left,
